name: Prophetic & Foundational Transmission (570-750 CE)
description: The Prophetic era and the generation of the Companions. The foundational transmission of interior knowledge through direct proximity to the Prophet Muhammad.
significance: This period establishes the spiritual roots from which all Sufi lineages claim descent.

name: Early Ascetic & Ethical Formation (750-900 CE)
description: The emergence of the first generation of named Sufi figures. Characterized by intense asceticism, ethical rigor, and the development of the vocabulary of the interior life.
significance: Key centers: Basra and Kufa. Key figures: Hasan al-Basri, Rabia al-Adawiyya.

name: Classical Theoretical Consolidation (900-1100 CE)
description: The systematic articulation of Sufi doctrine. Production of the great classical manuals, theoretical frameworks for maqamat and ahwal.
significance: Key figures: Junayd of Baghdad, Hallaj. Production of classical manuals.

name: Institutional Tariqa Formation (1100-1300 CE)
description: The crystallization of individual masters' teaching styles into formal orders with distinct methodologies, initiation systems, and silsilas.
significance: Foundation of the classical tariqa system. Key founders: Abdul Qadir Gilani, Ahmad Yasawi.

name: Metaphysical & Philosophical Expansion (1300-1500 CE)
description: The era of Ibn Arabi and the elaboration of Wahdat al-Wujud. Philosophical Sufism reaches its greatest systematic expression.
significance: Ibn Arabi's formation and writings. Rumi and the Mevleviyya.

name: Imperial & Global Expansion (1500-1800 CE)
description: The spread of Sufi orders across the Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires. Sufi culture becomes embedded in imperial court life.
significance: Sufi culture becomes embedded in imperial court life, urban governance, and rural missionary activity.

name: Reform, Renewal & Modern Rearticulation (1800-Present)
description: Responses to European colonialism, modernism, and internal theological critique. Reform movements seek to purify practice.
significance: Emergence of diaspora Sufi communities in the West. Key movements: Sanusiyya, Tijaniyya.
